nodes.name: New Britain thicketbird
nodes.summary: The New Britain thicketbird or Bismarck thicketbird (Cincloramphus grosvenori) is a bird species. It used to be placed in the "Old World warbler" family Sylviidae, but it does not seem to be a close relative of the typical warblers; probably it belongs in the grass warbler family Locustellidae. It is found only in the rarely visited highlands of the island of New Britain in Papua New Guinea. This little-known species was for long classified as a data deficient species by the IUCN, due to the general lack of reliable data on its distribution and numbers. While there have been no records since its discovery in 1959, recent evidence suggests the habitat destruction in the Bismarck Archipelago presents a greater risk than previously believed, leading to the Bismarck thicketbird being listed as a Vulnerable species in the 2008 red list. It used to be considered conspecific with the Santo thicketbird and the Bougainville thicketbird. Footnotes References BirdLife International (BLI) (2008): 2008 IUCN Redlist status changes. Retrieved 2008-MAY-23. External links BirdLife Species Factsheet.

nodes.name: Cincloramphus
nodes.summary: Cincloramphus（Cincloramphus）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联12种鸟类，如New Britain thicketbird、Rusty thicketbird、黄斑草莺等12种鸟类。

nodes.name: VU
nodes.summary: VU表示 IUCN 易危等级，当前关联694种鸟类，如丹顶鹤、索马里鸵鸟、大斑几维鸟等694种鸟类。
